stimulus: Yiyun Li's story collection A Thousand Years of Good Prayers features multiple stories about family members struggling to understand one another. In "The Princess of Nebraska," a young expectant mother considers her uncertain future while drifting through the streets of Chicago with a near-stranger. ______blank in the title story, a retired father visits his adult daughter in the American Midwest and finds that years of unspoken distance are not easily bridged in conversation.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. "Similarly" indicates the addition of a related, parallel idea. In this sentence, the author is providing another comparable story to the one discussed in the previous sentence. Therefore, "similarly" works best in this context.

Choice B is incorrect. "Of course" introduces a concession or acknowledgment, but the text is not conceding anything; both examples support the opening claim. Choice C is incorrect. "For example" introduces an example, but the second story is not an example of the first—it is another, comparable story. Choice D is incorrect. "Therefore" indicates cause and effect, but the second story is not caused by the first; both simply illustrate the opening claim about the collection.
